start.speaker: System
start.text: Welcome to ROI - Return on Investment. Choose your quest from the map to begin your financial journey.

lvl1_scene1.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl1_scene1.text: Yay! I got ₹5000 as my pocket money this month.

lvl1_father_rd.speaker: Father
lvl1_father_rd.text: That's great, but remember—you shouldn't spend it all. You should save a part of it in an RD, a recurring deposit. It will help you in the future.

lvl1_rd_explain.speaker: Father
lvl1_rd_explain.text: An RD, or Recurring Deposit, is a way of saving money regularly. You deposit a fixed amount every month for a certain period of time. The bank gives you interest, so your total amount grows over time and builds a habit of disciplined saving.

choices.text: I understand! Let's plan my month.

lvl1_choice_month.speaker: System
lvl1_choice_month.text: You have ₹5000 this month. Choose how much you want to allocate to Savings (RD) and Expenditure.

choices.mentorText: Excellent! You're following the RD advice. Saving ₹3000 is a big step!
Saving a little is better than nothing, but will it be enough for your future goals?

lvl1_party_invite.speaker: Friends
lvl1_party_invite.text: Hey! We're going for a party this weekend, are you coming? (It sounds fun… but should I spend my money on this?)

choices.mentorText: Balance is key, but don't blow your budget!
Great self-control! This money can go towards your bigger goals.

lvl1_trip_info.speaker: Friends
lvl1_trip_info.text: By the way, we're planning a trip after a few months! You should start saving from now so you can join us.

choices.text: Yes, I want to start saving for the trip!
choices.mentorText: Sinking funds are great for planned expenses like trips!
If you don't start now, you might miss it!

lvl1_college_needs.speaker: Teacher
lvl1_college_needs.text: You have been assigned a project. Make sure you complete it on time. (I'll need to buy materials. This is a NEED. Money will be spent from your budget.)

choices.mentorText: Always account for your project needs.

lvl1_summary.speaker: System
lvl1_summary.text: Time period completed. Here is your summary for the semester.

choices.text: Am I going on the trip?

lvl1_trip_outcome.speaker: System
lvl1_trip_outcome.text: Trip day is here! Let's see if your savings were enough...

lvl2_scene1.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl2_scene1.text: I just received my first salary — ₹50,000!

lvl2_father_call.speaker: Father
lvl2_father_call.text: Hello beta, got your first salary? I'm proud of you. But now comes the important part—managing it. Try dividing your money into three parts—needs, wants, and savings.

choices.text: So I just have to balance it?

lvl2_choice_month.speaker: System
lvl2_choice_month.text: You received ₹50,000 this month. How would you like to use it?

choices.mentorText: Aggressive saving early on builds a massive safety net!
A healthy balance for a young professional.

lvl2_emi_offer.speaker: Friend
lvl2_emi_offer.text: A new phone just launched. You can get it on ₹5,000 EMI.

lvl2_emi_talk.speaker: Father
lvl2_emi_talk.text: Thinking of buying something? Small payments feel easy, but they continue every month. Better think before saying yes.

choices.mentorText: EMIs are debt. They limit your future freedom.
Avoiding unnecessary debt is the first rule of wealth.

lvl2_quiz_intro.speaker: System
lvl2_quiz_intro.text: Let's try a quick activity. Select whether each is a NEED or a WANT.

choices.text: Medical is NEED, Outing is WANT
choices.mentorText: Level 2 Cleared! You've received a bonus and are moving to Level 3.

lvl3_scene1.speaker: Friend
lvl3_scene1.text: I'll pay, you send me your part. Wait, I'll just use my card. Use credit, it's easier. (Debit or credit... what's the real difference?)

lvl3_father_cards.speaker: Father
lvl3_father_cards.text: Debit uses your own money from your account. Credit lets you spend now and pay later. It's useful, but only if you stay in control.

lvl3_bank_savings.speaker: Elder Brother
lvl3_bank_savings.text: Most of my money just sits in my account... Does it even do anything? That's your savings account beta, it earns interest. It grows slowly.

lvl3_invest_intro.speaker: Friend
lvl3_invest_intro.text: Bro I invested and made profit! In mutual funds, stocks... stuff like that. I want to start investing... but how?

lvl3_invest_options.speaker: Father
lvl3_invest_options.text: Start simple. FD is safe. Mutual Funds are managed. Stocks are risky. More return = more risk. You choose based on comfort.

lvl3_invest_choice.speaker: System
lvl3_invest_choice.text: You have ₹20,000 savings. What will you do?

lvl3_lapse.speaker: System
lvl3_lapse.text: 3 months later... Let's see how your investments performed.

lvl3_realisation.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl3_realisation.text: Saving keeps money safe. Credit needs control. But investing helps money grow! I made financial choices and saw their impact.
